form.label: Settings
form.description: Bootstrap defaults applied to NEW entities at discovery time, app health checks, and the audit log of inline edits. Existing entities keep their per-row thresholds until you edit them.

div.p: Not sure what numbers to put here? Click Use recommended defaults: default_monitored=0, default_max_latency_seconds=600, default_max_gap_seconds=3600, default_monitoring_schedule=247. Existing rows are left alone; only newly discovered entities pick these up.

search.query: | inputlookup carbide_settings_lookup | sort + setting_key | table _key, setting_key, setting_value

div.label:
Key
default_monitored (0 or 1) default_max_latency_seconds default_max_gap_seconds default_monitoring_schedule (247|weekdays|business_hours) business_hours_start (HHMM, e.g. 800) business_hours_end (HHMM, e.g. 1600) business_timezone (IANA, e.g. Europe/Ljubljana)
Value

html.p: Settings stored here are read by discovery searches at run time via the carbide_setting_num / carbide_setting_str macros and override the corresponding carbide_default_* macros. Changes apply to newly discovered entities only; existing rows keep their per-row thresholds.

search.query: | makeresults | eval check="carbide_settings collection reachable" | append [ | inputlookup carbide_settings_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_settings collection reachable" ] | stats max(rows) as rows by check | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows), "ok", "fail") | append [ | makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_hosts_lookup | stats count as rows ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_tracked_hosts collection reachable" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows), "ok", "fail") ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_sources_lookup | stats count as rows ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_tracked_sources collection reachable" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows), "ok", "fail") ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_entity_filters_lookup | stats count as rows ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_entity_filters collection reachable (seeded if > 0)" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows) AND rows>0, "ok", if(isnotnull(rows), "warn (empty)", "fail")) ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_entity_filters_seed | stats count as rows ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_entity_filters_seed CSV present" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows) AND rows>0, "ok", "fail") ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | tstats count where index=`carbide_index` earliest=-7d@d ] | stats max(count) as rows | eval check="dedicated index `carbide_index` reachable + recent snapshots" | eval status = case(rows>0, "ok", isnotnull(rows), "warn (no events in 7d)", 1=1, "fail") ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | tstats count as rows where `carbide_entity_filter("hosts")` earliest=-1h@h ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_entity_filter(\"hosts\") macro returns a valid clause" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows), "ok", "fail") ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_holidays_lookup | stats count as rows ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_holidays collection reachable" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows), "ok", "fail") ] | append [ | makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_assets_lookup | stats count as rows ] | stats max(rows) as rows | eval check="carbide_assets collection reachable" | eval status = if(isnotnull(rows), "ok", "fail") ] | where isnotnull(check) | table check, status, rows

| rest /servicesNS/-/carbide_app_for_splunk/saved/searches splunk_server=local | search title="Carbide -*" | eval next_run = if(isnull(next_scheduled_time) OR next_scheduled_time="", "n/a", next_scheduled_time) | table title cron_schedule disabled is_scheduled next_run | rename title as "saved search"

| makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_hosts_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_tracked_hosts" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_sources_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_tracked_sources" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_settings_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_settings" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_entity_filters_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_entity_filters" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_status_history_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_status_history" ] | where isnotnull(collection) | table collection rows

panel.title: Audit trail (every KV write under this app)

html.p: Sourced from Splunk's built-in splunkd access log (index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access) — every REST call to the Carbide collections is recorded automatically with the caller and the URI. No app-side audit code required.

search.query: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access uri_path="*/storage/collections/data/carbide_*" (method=POST OR method=DELETE) | eval segments = split(uri_path, "/") | eval last_seg = mvindex(segments, -1) | eval prev_seg = mvindex(segments, -2) | eval is_root_post = if(method="POST" AND prev_seg="data", 1, 0) | eval is_batch = if(last_seg="batch_save", 1, 0) | eval action = case( method="DELETE", "delete", is_batch=1, "bulk_save", is_root_post=1, "create", method="POST", "update", 1=1, method) | eval collection = case( is_batch=1, mvindex(segments, -3), is_root_post=1, last_seg, 1=1, prev_seg) | eval kv_key = case( is_batch=1 OR is_root_post=1, "-", 1=1, last_seg) | sort - _time | head 200 | table _time, user, action, collection, kv_key, status, uri_path | rename kv_key as "_key", uri_path as "URI"
